name: Nazava Water Filter
description: Nazava is a two bucket system ceramic water filter with a storage capacity of 2*6 liters.

taxonomy.sub_category: Nazava Water Filter Updated on December 7, 2023 · Created on June 17, 2014 Nazava Water Filter Open-source Nazava is a two bucket system ceramic water filter with a storage capacity of 2*6 liters. Developed By Nazava Tested By Nazava Water Filter Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Nazava Transparent Water Filter is a two bucket water filter system that was mainly developed as an emergency relief water filtration kit by Lieselotte Heederik. Currently, this product is also being used as a household water filter system in many parts of Indonesia. The water is suitable for a single household and the product provides a storage capacity of 2*6 L and a flow rate of 2-3 L/hr. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 1: No Poverty Market Suggested Retail Price $20.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Sales are primarily carried out by a network of 122 local resellers who sell in different parts of the country. This product is also being commercialized by diverse NGOs and through direct sales. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Colempaques ceramic candle filter , Ekofil filter , Berkey Ceramic Filters , Katadyn Drip , Stéfani Filtro Flex , and Arcifiltro . Countries Ethiopia, Indonesia Manufacturing/Building Method The ceramic candles are made locally in Indonesia. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Users can obtain the product by ordering it from Nazava’s website or purchasing it from several local resellers. Distributions to Date Status As of 2020, over 145,000 units have been distributed. Type of filter Ceramic candle filter Primary material of construction Diatomaceous earth, nano silver, and activated carbon. Additives No additives Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 2-3 L/hr Bacteria reduction 4 log Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction 4.5 Log (>99.995%) Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Iron removal up to 90% and Manganese removal up to 50 %. Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unkown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Maintenance schedule Filters are recommended for scrubbing when flow rate slows down and that is usually once every two weeks. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 7,000 L Safe water storage integration 2*6 liters Design Specifications The Nazava Transparent Water Filter is a two bucket ceramic water filter system. It is mainly designed for a household of 4 people. The specifications of the filter include: Volume of storage: 2*6 L Flow speed: 2-3 L/hour Capacity: 7000 L before replacement Material of container: Polyethylene plastic Mechanism of water filter: Ceramic filtration with impregnation of silver to kill germs and Activated carbon to improve the taste Pore size: 0.4 micron. Product Schematics Technical Support The manufacturer provides online training videos on proper use and maintenance of the units. Replacement Components Available for purchase from the retailer. Lifecycle Nazava provides a 1-year warranty with the filter capacity of up to 7,000 liters of water. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters This filter was designed to reduce bacteria by 99.99%, >99.99% protozoa, and 99% turbidity. The product was also designed to provide a flow rate of 2-3 Liters per hour. Vetted Performance Status According to laboratory testing carried by WHO, the Nazava Water Filter protects against bacteria and protozoa only. Safety No known safety risks if used properly. Complementary Technical Systems None Academic Research and References Chaudhuri, M., Verma, S. R., & Gupta, A.,1994, Performance evaluation of ceramic filter candles, Journal of Environmental Engineering, 120(6), 1646-1651. Zievers, J. F., & Zievers, E. C.,1993 , Reinforced ceramic filter candle and method of making same, U.S. Patent No. 5,223,138. Washington, DC: U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. Carlsen, M., & Waddell, K.,2016, Nazava Water Filters; Social Impact Assessment. Holtslag, H., & Mc Gill, J. Practical ideas to reach SDG6 . Cadeddu, S. B., Donovan, J. D., Topple, C., De Waal, G. A., & Masli, E. K. (2019), Frugal Innovation and the New Product Development Process: Insights from Indonesia , Routledge. “Our Impact,” Nazava Water Filters International, 08-Sep-2021. Available: https://global.nazava.com/impact/our-impact/ Goal 6. (n.d.). Sdgs.Un.Org. available: https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 “Where To Buy,” Nazava Water Filters International, 08-Sep-2021. Available: https://global.nazava.com/support/where-to-buy/ “Nazava Water Filters. Water that Cares,” Nazava Water Filters International, 18-Sep-2020. Available: https://global.nazava.com/ “What can be removed from drinking water by Nazava Water Filters?,” Nazava Water Filters International, 13-Oct-2021. Available: https://global.nazava.com/product/what-can-be-removed-from-drinking-water-by-nazava-water-filters/ J. Kolonel Masturi, “Nazava Water Filter,” Nazava.com. Available: https://www.nazava.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/WHO-Product-report-Nazava.pdf G. van Hofwegen, “Laboratory Test Results Nazava Water Filters,” Nazava, 19-Jul-2018. Available: https://www.nazava.com/laboratory-test-results-nazava-water-filters/ Compliance with regulations Manufacturer states that Nazava complies with the drinking water standard of WHO for the microbial test mainly bacteria and protozoa removal. Evaluation methods Filters have been tested in laboratories worldwide. Other Information Nazava Water Filters received the 2013 Laureate of The Tech Awards and was the winner of the 2016 Ashden Award for Sustainable Energy and Water.
